query:
You are a SaaS pricing analyst. For each of the 10 companies below, extract their CURRENT pricing structure from their public pricing pages ({domain}/pricing, {domain}/plans), G2, Capterra, and reverse-engineering posts.

Companies:
  1. name="Nalo", website="https://nalo.fr"
  2. name="Linxo", website="https://linxo.com"
  3. name="Grisbee", website="https://grisbee.com"
  4. name="Horiz.io", website="https://horiz.io"
  5. name="Sharesight", website="https://sharesight.com"
  6. name="InvMon", website="https://invmon.com"
  7. name="Wealthfolio", website="https://ealthfolio.app"
  8. name="Quicken", website="https://quicken.com"
  9. name="Merlin", website="https://merlin.com"
  10. name="Sure", website="https://sure.com"

For EACH company extract:
- pricing_model_kind: one of Freemium, Subscription, Usage-based, Enterprise, Hybrid
- free_plan: true/false
- starts_at_usd: lowest paid tier monthly USD (0 if free plan only, null if Enterprise-only / contact sales)
- mention: short UI tagline e.g. 'Starts at $20/user/mo' or 'Contact sales' or 'Free + paid plans from $8/mo'
- tiers: array of {name, price_monthly_usd, price_annual_usd, features (3-6 strings), target}. Include ALL public tiers (e.g. Free, Pro, Business, Enterprise). For Enterprise tiers without public price, set price_monthly_usd=null and target='Enterprise'.
- recent_changes: any pricing change in last 12 months (date + source). Leave blank if none.

Omit any field you cannot verify — do NOT invent prices. Return one object per company in the SAME order as the input list. Preserve company names EXACTLY.
answer:
{"executive_summary": "- **Dominant Freemium Model**: 6 of 10 companies (Linxo, Horiz.io, Sharesight, InvMon, Wealthfolio, Merlin) use a Freemium model with a functional free tier and paid upgrades -> prioritize free-tier UX as the primary conversion lever.
- **Usage-Based Anomaly**: Nalo and Grisbee charge percentage-of-AUM fees instead of flat subscriptions -> these platforms monetize proportionally to customer success, aligning incentives but limiting revenue predictability.
- **Enterprise-Only Opaque Pricing**: Sure offers no public pricing, requiring sales engagement -> increases deal friction but supports high-ACV contracts typical of insurtech infrastructure.
- **Quicken's Aggressive Promo-to-Renewal Gap**: Simplifi jumps from $2.99/mo first-year to $5.99/mo at renewal (100% increase); Classic tiers see ~67% renewal hikes -> subscribers face significant sticker shock, driving churn risk documented in community forums.
- **Wealthfolio's Open-Core Disruption**: Core app is fully free and open-source; only optional Connect tier ($7.99/mo) adds cloud/broker sync -> undercuts subscription-only competitors on price while monetizing convenience.
- **InvMon's Unique Hybrid Licensing**: Offers both a one-time $199 lifetime license AND a $4.99/mo subscription, rare in a market dominated by subscription-only -> appeals to privacy-conscious users who reject recurring billing.
- **Sharesight's Granular Tiering**: Five tiers from Free (10 holdings) to Business (custom) with per-holding/per-portfolio limits -> precise segmentation captures value from casual investors to professional advisors.
- **Merlin's AI Model Bundling**: Pro at $19/mo bundles GPT-5.2, Claude Opus, Gemini, and Grok under one subscription -> undercuts buying each AI model separately, but Fair Use caps create uncertainty.
- **European Platforms Favor EUR Pricing**: Nalo, Linxo, Grisbee, and Horiz.io price in EUR, creating FX opacity for USD-based comparison -> USD equivalents are approximate and subject to exchange-rate fluctuation.
- **Limited Recent Pricing Changes**: Only Quicken shows documented price increases in the last 12 months (2025 community reports); the other 9 companies show no publicly documented changes -> stability for now, but Quicken's hikes may signal industry trend.

---", "1_nalo": "**Website**: https://nalo.fr  
**pricing_model_kind**: Usage-based  
**free_plan**: false  
**starts_at_usd**: null  
**mention**: "Fees from 1.35%/yr on AUM"

**tiers**:

| Name | price_monthly_usd | price_annual_usd | Features | Target |
|------|-------------------|-------------------|----------|--------|
| Nalo Patrimoine | null (AUM-based) | ~1.65% of AUM | Assurance-vie, 0 EUR entry/transfer fees, ETF allocation (avg 0.25%), automated portfolio management | Individual investors |
| Nalo Flex | null (AUM-based) | ~1.65% of AUM | Assurance-vie + SCPI access (max 3% fees), 0 EUR entry/transfer fees, automated management | Investors seeking diversification |
| Nalo PER | null (AUM-based) | ~1.60% of AUM | Retirement plan, tax-deductible contributions, lowest mandate fee (0.90%), 0 EUR entry/transfer fees | Retirement savers |

Nalo is a French robo-advisor that charges no flat subscription. Fees are deducted quarterly from contract value as a percentage of AUM. The mandate fee ranges from 0.90% (Patrimoine/PER) to 0.95% (Flex), plus insurer fees, with total annual fees capped at 1.60-1.65%. Zero entry fees, zero transfer fees, zero performance fees, and zero arbitration fees are emphasized as competitive differentiators. A referral program (Nalo Ambassadeur) grants a 3-month fee waiver to both referrer and referee. No publicly documented pricing changes in the last 12 months.

**recent_changes**:

Sources: [1_nalo[0]] [12], [1_nalo[1]] [11]

---", "2_linxo": "**Website**: https://linxo.com  
**pricing_model_kind**: Freemium  
**free_plan**: true  
**starts_at_usd**: ~3.23  
**mention**: "Free + Premium from ~EUR 2.99/mo (annual billing)"

**tiers**:

| Name | price_monthly_usd | price_annual_usd | Features | Target |
|------|-------------------|-------------------|----------|--------|
| Free | 0 | 0 | Bank account aggregation, automatic transaction categorization, customizable spending alerts, basic budget, 2FA security | Casual budgeters |
| Premium | ~3.23 (EUR 2.99/mo annual) | ~38.75 (EUR 35.88/yr) | Detailed analytical reports, advanced budget customization, data export, full cash-flow analysis | Active financial managers |

Linxo is a French personal finance management app classified as Freemium on app stores. The free tier aggregates bank accounts and auto-categorizes transactions but is intentionally limited, pushing serious users toward Premium. The Premium subscription is billed annually at approximately EUR 2.99/month (EUR 35.88/year), per the 2026 review on francaiseapps.fr. An earlier 2019 Finovate source cited EUR 4.49/month or EUR 29.99/year, suggesting pricing has been restructured. The review rates value-for-money at only 5/10, criticizing the paywall on core analytical features. Credit Agricole Group acquired a majority stake in Linxo Group in January 2020.

**recent_changes**:

Sources: [2_linxo[0]] [72], [2_linxo[1]] [55], [2_linxo[2]] [2], [2_linxo[3]] [56]

---", "3_grisbee": "**Website**: https://grisbee.com (redirects to grisbee.fr)  
**pricing_model_kind**: Hybrid  
**free_plan**: true  
**starts_at_usd**: 0  
**mention**: "Free tool + advisory fees on AUM"

**tiers**:

| Name | price_monthly_usd | price_annual_usd | Features | Target |
|------|-------------------|-------------------|----------|--------|
| Free Tool | 0 | 0 | Online wealth management dashboard, portfolio overview, financial planning tools, asset tracking | Self-directed investors |
| Advisory Services | null (AUM-based) | Variable % of AUM | Personalized financial advice, placement recommendations, patrimoine management, competitive fees vs traditional advisors | Investors seeking guidance |

Grisbee is a French online wealth management platform. The core tool for tracking and managing patrimoine is accessible at no cost. Revenue comes from advisory and placement services, which charge percentage-based fees on AUM - described as "particularly competitive" compared to private banks and traditional wealth management cabinets. The platform emphasizes accessibility of quality financial advice and placements for all users. Specific AUM fee percentages are not publicly itemized on the tarifs page in the same way as Nalo; instead, Grisbee describes its fee structure as transparent and competitive without publishing a detailed schedule. No publicly documented pricing changes in the last 12 months.

**recent_changes**:

Sources: [3_grisbee[0]] [24], [3_grisbee[1]] [97]

---", "4_horiz_io": "**Website**: https://horiz.io  
**pricing_model_kind**: Freemium  
**free_plan**: true  
**starts_at_usd**: ~8.91  
**mention**: "Free + plans from EUR 8.25/mo (annual)"

**tiers**:

| Name | price_monthly_usd | price_annual_usd | Features | Target |
|------|-------------------|-------------------|----------|--------|
| Free | 0 | 0 | Basic yield simulations (limited), rental yield calculator | Casual property researchers |
| Standard | ~21.59 (EUR 19.99/mo) or ~8.91/mo annual | ~106.92 (EUR 99/yr) | 30 simulations/month, location nue/meublee/LMNP/SCI, exclusive property search, printable investment report, unlimited property management (up to 50 units), 1 digital lease, tax declaration assistance | Individual real estate investors |
| PRO | null | null | Advanced tools for real estate professionals, custom features, priority support | Real estate agents & agencies |

Horiz.io (formerly Rendement Locatif) is a French rental yield calculation and property management platform. The paid Standard plan offers both monthly (EUR 19.99) and annual (EUR 8.25/mo, billed at EUR 99/year) billing, representing a 58% discount on annual commitment. The platform notes that 30% of the subscription price is tax-deductible. The PRO tier requires contacting sales and targets real estate professionals with advanced tools. Founded in 2016 by Bassel Abedi. No publicly documented pricing changes in the last 12 months.

**recent_changes**:

Sources: [4_horiz_io[0]] [7], [4_horiz_io[1]] [6]

---", "5_sharesight": "**Website**: https://sharesight.com  
**pricing_model_kind**: Freemium  
**free_plan**: true  
**starts_at_usd**: 7  
**mention**: "Free + paid plans from $7/mo"

**tiers**:

| Name | price_monthly_usd | price_annual_usd | Features | Target |
|------|-------------------|-------------------|----------|--------|
| Free | 0 | 0 | 1 portfolio, 10 holdings, 1 custom group, basic reports (Performance, Sold Securities, All Trades) | Beginners |
| Starter | 9.33 (monthly) | 84 (7/mo billed annually) | 1 portfolio, 30 holdings, 3 custom groups, Diversity/Exposure reports, file attachments, historical cost data | Simple portfolio management |
| Standard | 24 (monthly) | 216 (18/mo billed annually) | 4 portfolios, unlimited holdings, 5 custom groups, cash accounts, label filtering, Xero integration, extended performance reporting | Active individual investors |
| Premium | 60 (monthly) | 540 (45/mo billed annually) | 10 portfolios, unlimited holdings, 10 custom groups, Multi-Currency Valuation, custom branding, advanced analytics, priority support | Advanced individual investors |
| Business | null | null (custom) | Multi-client management, advisor dashboard, bulk reporting, API access, white-label options, custom integrations | Accountants & financial advisors |

Sharesight is an award-winning online portfolio tracker and reporting tool. Annual billing provides approximately 25% savings versus monthly across all paid tiers. All plans include global market coverage, automatic dividend tracking, price alerts, trade confirmation email imports, and mobile app access. The Business plan is the enterprise tier requiring custom pricing. SOC 2 certified. No publicly documented pricing changes in the last 12 months.

**recent_changes**:

Sources: [5_sharesight[0]] [17], [5_sharesight[1]] [20], [5_sharesight[2]] [77]

---", "6_invmon": "**Website**: https://invmon.com  
**pricing_model_kind**: Freemium  
**free_plan**: true  
**starts_at_usd**: 4.99  
**mention**: "Free + paid from $4.99/mo or $199 lifetime"

**tiers**:

| Name | price_monthly_usd | price_annual_usd | Features | Target |
|------|-------------------|-------------------|----------|--------|
| Free | 0 | 0 | 1 broker account, 12 positions, custom instruments, account groups | Basic users |
| Lifetime | null (one-time) | 199 (one-time) | Unlimited accounts, unlimited positions, account groups & sub-totals, priority support, feature upgrades, Pro Features | Privacy-focused power users |
| Subscription | 4.99 | 49.99 | All Lifetime features + ongoing subscription-only updates, 2-week free trial, cancel anytime | Users wanting latest features |

InvMon is a desktop investment monitoring application with local data storage (no cloud dependency). The Free plan is available "for an unlimited time." The Lifetime license is a one-time $199 payment granting perpetual use of that version's features; subscription-only features require an active subscription. The Subscription plan at $4.99/month or $49.99/year includes all Lifetime features plus ongoing updates. All prices are in USD; sales tax may apply. Notable for its privacy-first design philosophy - all data stays local. No publicly documented pricing changes in the last 12 months.

**recent_changes**:

Sources: [6_invmon[0]] [28], [6_invmon[1]] [29], [6_invmon[2]] [30]

---", "7_wealthfolio": "**Website**: https://wealthfolio.app (input listed as https://ealthfolio.app - likely typo)  
**pricing_model_kind**: Freemium  
**free_plan**: true  
**starts_at_usd**: 7.99  
**mention**: "Free + Connect from $7.99/mo"

**tiers**:

| Name | price_monthly_usd | price_annual_usd | Features | Target |
|------|-------------------|-------------------|----------|--------|
| Free | 0 | 0 | Portfolio tracking, AI assistant, retirement/FIRE planner, income tracking, local data storage, unlimited portfolios & accounts | Self-hosted/privacy-focused investors |
| Connect | 7.99 | null (not publicly disclosed) | Broker sync (automatic data pull), end-to-end encrypted multi-device sync, Household View for family sharing | Users wanting automation & cloud convenience |

Wealthfolio is an open-source, local-first desktop investment tracker. The core application is completely free with no account required, no subscriptions, and no cloud dependency. It runs on desktop, mobile, and self-hosted web servers with local data storage. The optional "Wealthfolio Connect" subscription enables automatic brokerage sync, encrypted cross-device sync, and Household View. The Connect tier starts at $7.99/month according to the official Questrade integration page, though the Toolradar pricing analysis notes the full Connect pricing schedule is not publicly disclosed and may be custom/quote-based. No publicly documented pricing changes in the last 12 months.

**recent_changes**:

Sources: [7_wealthfolio[0]] [83], [7_wealthfolio[1]] [86], [7_wealthfolio[2]] [42], [7_wealthfolio[3]] [45], [7_wealthfolio[4]] [60]

---", "8_quicken": "**Website**: https://quicken.com  
**pricing_model_kind**: Subscription  
**free_plan**: false  
**starts_at_usd**: 3.99  
**mention**: "Starts at $3.99/mo (billed annually)"

**tiers**:

| Name | price_monthly_usd | price_annual_usd | Features | Target |
|------|-------------------|-------------------|----------|--------|
| Simplifi | 5.99 (renewal); 2.99 first-year promo | 71.88 (renewal); 35.88 first-year | 14,000+ financial institution connections, predictive spending plan, custom savings goals, investment tracking (IRR & TWR), retirement planner | Personal finance (web & mobile) |
| Classic Deluxe | 4.99 (renewal); 2.99 first-year promo | 59.88 (renewal); 35.88 first-year | Desktop personal finance, bill & income tracking, custom budgets, tax planning, investment tracking | Desktop personal finance users |
| Classic Premier | 6.99 (renewal); 4.19 first-year promo | 83.88 (renewal); 50.28 first-year | All Deluxe features + advanced investment tracking, stock quotes, Schedule D tax reports, rental property tracking | Advanced investors |
| Classic Business & Personal | 9.99 (renewal); 5.99 first-year promo | 119.88 (renewal); 71.88 first-year | All Premier features + business & rental tracking, invoicing, Stripe integration, Schedules C/E/F tax reporting | Self-employed & small business |
| Business & Personal (Web & Mobile) | 7.99 (renewal); 3.99 first-year promo | 95.88 (renewal); 47.88 first-year | Built-in invoicing, Stripe payments, tax reporting (Schedules C, E, F), personal + business finance tracking | Self-employed (cloud-based) |

Quicken offers no free plan; all subscriptions include a 30-day money-back guarantee. All plans are billed annually with no monthly payment option. First-year promotional prices offer 40-50% off for new subscribers (promo deadline July 4, 2026 per official page). Renewal rates are significantly higher: Simplifi sees a 100% increase from first-year to renewal; Classic tiers see approximately 67% increases. Community users have reported further cumulative increases beyond the first renewal cycle - one user documented a 42% cumulative increase over two years. The official pricing page states "starting at $3.99/mo" for the lowest tier.

**recent_changes**: 2025 price increases reported across Quicken community forums. Users reported renewal prices doubling vs prior-year rates. Simplifi community discussion documented 50%+ increases on subscription renewals. Sources: [8_quicken[0]] [74], [8_quicken[1]] [76], [8_quicken[2]] [92], [8_quicken[3]] [93]

Sources: [8_quicken[4]] [96], [8_quicken[2]] [92], [8_quicken[5]] [91]

---", "9_merlin": "**Website**: https://merlin.com (note: Merlin AI's pricing page is at https://getmerlin.in/pricing)  
**pricing_model_kind**: Freemium  
**free_plan**: true  
**starts_at_usd**: 19  
**mention**: "Free + Pro from $19/mo"

**tiers**:

| Name | price_monthly_usd | price_annual_usd | Features | Target |
|------|-------------------|-------------------|----------|--------|
| Free | 0 | 0 | 102 basic messages/day, 10 premium model messages/day, 1 image generation/day (premium models), 100MB vault, limited AI tools, Chrome extension, sync across devices | Casual AI users |
| Pro | 19 | 228 | 50x premium model usage, Deep Research (5/mo), 5GB vault, 2500 images/mo, video generation, AI humanizer (250K chars/mo), unlimited essay writer, 24x7 priority support, 100K token context | Power users & professionals |
| Teams | 15/seat | 180/seat (min 2 seats) | All Pro features + team management, admin dashboard, larger context windows, SOC2/GDPR/ISO 27001 compliance, centralized usage controls | Businesses & teams |

Merlin AI is an all-in-one AI assistant Chrome extension and web app that bundles access to models from OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, DeepSeek, and xAI under one subscription. The Free plan provides limited daily queries across premium models (GPT-5.2, Claude Haiku 4.5, Gemini 3.0, Grok 4.1 limited to 10/day). Pro unlocks 50x usage on premium models with monthly caps (e.g., 5,000 GPT-5.2 messages, 1,000 Claude Sonnet 4.6, 500 Claude Opus 4.6). Fair Use policy applies; built-in top-up feature allows purchasing additional credits. Annual billing saves 35% compared to monthly. Some third-party sites list older tier names (Starter $19, Boost $39, Elite $119) that do not appear on the current official pricing page. No publicly documented pricing changes in the last 12 months.

**recent_changes**:

Sources: [9_merlin[0]] [52], [9_merlin[1]] [37], [9_merlin[2]] [88], Merlin AI on eesel AI

---", "10_sure": "**Website**: https://sure.com  
**pricing_model_kind**: Enterprise  
**free_plan**: false  
**starts_at_usd**: null  
**mention**: "Contact sales"

**tiers**:

| Name | price_monthly_usd | price_annual_usd | Features | Target |
|------|-------------------|-------------------|----------|--------|
| Enterprise | null | null | AI-powered underwriting, rating engine, billing schedules, claims processing, refunds & MTAs, white-label insurance programs, API access | Insurance carriers, brands, MGAs, TPAs |

Note: sure.com is the domain for a telecom provider operating in Guernsey and the Isle of Man. The SaaS product most relevant to this analysis is listed under sureapp.com ("Sure - The AI Layer for Intelligent Insurance"), an enterprise insurtech platform, and also appears on Capterra as SURE (subscription/revenue management software by Magnaquest). Both the insurtech platform and the Capterra-listed SURE software use contact-sales-only pricing with no public tiers, no free trial, and no free plan. The insurtech platform's primary CTAs are "Talk to Sure" and "Contact sales." The Capterra listing shows "Get Price" / "Contact vendor" as the pricing option. No publicly documented pricing changes in the last 12 months.

**recent_changes**:

Sources: [10_sure[0]] [65], [10_sure[1]] [32], [10_sure[2]] [79]

---", "comparative_analysis": "| Company | Model | Free Plan | Lowest Paid (USD/mo) | Billing Currency | Tier Count |
|---------|-------|-----------|----------------------|------------------|------------|
| Nalo | Usage-based | No | null (AUM %) | EUR | 3 (contract types) |
| Linxo | Freemium | Yes | ~3.23 | EUR | 2 |
| Grisbee | Hybrid | Yes | 0 (free tool; advisory is AUM %) | EUR | 2 |
| Horiz.io | Freemium | Yes | ~8.91 (annual) | EUR | 3 |
| Sharesight | Freemium | Yes | 7 | USD | 5 |
| InvMon | Freemium | Yes | 4.99 | USD | 3 |
| Wealthfolio | Freemium | Yes | 7.99 | USD | 2 |
| Quicken | Subscription | No | 3.99 | USD | 5+ |
| Merlin | Freemium | Yes | 19 | USD | 3 |
| Sure | Enterprise | No | null | USD | 1 (contact sales) |

The lowest entry point among paid plans is Linxo at approximately $3.23/mo, followed by Quicken at $3.99/mo (promotional first-year) and InvMon at $4.99/mo. The highest freemium starting price is Merlin at $19/mo, reflecting its bundling of multiple premium AI model subscriptions. Usage-based and enterprise-only platforms (Nalo, Grisbee, Sure) cannot be directly compared on a per-month basis.

---", "synthesis": "Three distinct monetization philosophies emerge across these 10 platforms. **Subscription purists** (Quicken, Merlin) charge flat recurring fees with tiered feature access - Quicken enforces annual-only billing with steep promo-to-renewal jumps, while Merlin bundles multiple AI APIs under a single seat license. **Freemium practitioners** (Linxo, Horiz.io, Sharesight, InvMon, Wealthfolio) offer functional free tiers as conversion funnels, but vary dramatically in free-tier generosity: Wealthfolio gives away essentially the entire product locally, while Linxo walls off core analytics behind Premium. **AUM-aligned platforms** (Nalo, Grisbee) and **enterprise-only vendors** (Sure) avoid publishing monthly rates entirely, aligning cost to value delivered rather than to seat count.

The tension between openness and monetization is most visible in Wealthfolio's open-core model versus Quicken's lock-in strategy. Wealthfolio's free local app with optional paid Connect ($7.99/mo) pressures competitors who charge for basic tracking, while Quicken's 100% renewal increase on Simplifi (from $2.99 to $5.99/mo) creates churn risk that community forums already document. InvMon's lifetime license ($199 one-time) represents a third path - ownership over rental - that appeals to the privacy-conscious desktop audience but sacrifices recurring revenue.

European platforms (Nalo, Linxo, Grisbee, Horiz.io) consistently price in EUR and target domestic French markets, making direct USD comparison approximate. None of these four have documented recent pricing changes, whereas US-based Quicken shows the most aggressive pricing motion in the group. This may reflect the relatively nascent state of French fintech SaaS monetization versus the mature US personal finance software market, where established players can leverage switching costs to raise renewal prices.

---"}
